name: Peach Pie with Almond Crumble Topping
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds firm, ripe, yellow fleshed free stone peaches
recipeInstructions:
To make topping: Combine flour, sugar and nutmeg in a mixing bowl; stir well to mix. Stir in sliced almonds and melted butter evenly. Set aside for 5 minutes, then break the mixture into 1/4 to 1/2-inch crumbs. Set aside while preparing filling.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and set racks at middle level. Roll out bottom crust and arrange it in a 9-inch Pyrex dish.
To make filling: Peel peaches by cutting a cross in the blossom end of each and dropping them into boiling water for 20-30 seconds. Remove with a slotted spoon or skimmer and drop into ice water. If skin does not slip off easily, remove it with a sharp stainless steel paring knife (use an extra peach). Gently hold each peeled peach over a bowl; make cuts from stem to blossom end, then about 3/4-inch to the right of the first cut along the outside. Angle the knife blade back so the cuts meet at the pit. Twist upward slightly as a wedge falls into the bowl. Repeat around the peach, cutting it into 8-10 wedges. Discard pits and repeat with remaining peaches.
Add all filling ingredients except butter to the peaches and gently stir with a rubber spatula. Pour into prepared pastry shell and smooth. Dot with butter.
Scatter crumb topping evenly over filling. Place pie on bottom rack of oven and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes. Lower temperature to 350°F (175°C) and move pie to middle rack. Bake another 30 minutes, or until crust and crumble are deep golden and juices bubble slightly. Cool on a rack and serve warm or room temperature.
